title: Deceitful Transfer
summary: A parked-outside surveillance PCAP captures an FTP gallery sync tied to suspected infidelity and hidden spending.
scenario: You are a private investigator hired by a woman who believes her husband has been cheating, spending thousands on OnlyFans, and stashing pictures through plain FTP. You park outside the house long enough to capture a packet trace of his session. The hard part is that the FTP traffic contains multiple passive data streams and decoy files: solvers need to reconstruct the right transfers, identify the meaningful image, and inspect that recovered JPEG for hidden appended data.
learningObjectives: Map FTP control traffic to multiple passive-mode data channels
Correlate a recovered text artifact with a second recovered media artifact
Carve hidden appended data from a transferred JPEG

artifacts.name: deceitful_transfer_<seed>.pcap
artifacts.purpose: Generated packet capture with multiple passive-mode FTP data streams

artifacts.name: incident_memo.txt
artifacts.purpose: Case note from the private-investigator surveillance setup

hints: More than one passive data stream matters in this capture.
One recovered text file points to which image deserves a closer look.

llmHandoff.scope: This folder contains the metadata, generator, and internal notes for the challenge.
llmHandoff.implementNext: Tune the FTP case story, decoy transfer set, or hidden JPEG payload while keeping the multi-stream reconstruction path intact.
llmHandoff.doNotDoYet: Do not put the flag directly into plaintext control-channel traffic.
